summary:
The combined therapy improves patient outcomes but doesn't affect overall cancer incidences; it primarily extends life expectancy without impacting how many individuals develop metastatic disease annually because current strategies aim mostly towards pre-onset risks reduction while novel drugs address already present conditions efficiently after diagnosis has been confirmed clinically or pathologically via biopsy results etc., thus maintaining consistent yearly case counts despite prolonged survivals reported under these newer regimens like those involving both chemo- & immuno-based approaches against aggressive skin malignancies such as malignant melanomas which have become increasingly common over recent decades globally thanks largely to better detection methods alongside enhanced understanding about genetic predispositions contributing significantly toward susceptibility among certain populations especially fair-skinned ones living closer proximity near equatorial zones where UV radiation exposure tends higher naturally compared other latitudes further away geographically speaking .
The improved prognosis achieved using chemotherapeutic agents along side immune checkpoint inhibitors helps extend median OS however since no significant change occurs concerning underlying etiology responsible for initiating tumor growth therefore total numbers affected remains relatively stable across time period studied indicating continued need robust public health initiatives focused around sun protection education , regular self-examinations coupled screening programs aimed detecting lesions suspicious enough warranting dermatological evaluation sooner thereby facilitating earlier interventions potentially curative when caught promptly during initial stages . To conclude there will be increased proportionality amongst survivors who live much longer periods relative past cohorts yet absolute count won’t budge substantially given nature of therapeutic advances described herein targeting symptomatic management versus preventative medicine paradigm shift needed addressing root cause mechanisms driving carcinogenesis process itself if one wishes truly curb rising trend observed recently within global context pertaining cutaneous neoplasms including deadly variants like uveal / ocular melanoma too requiring multidisciplinary approach integrating basic science research findings into translational pipeline aiming discovery next generation targeted modalities capable eradicating precancerous cells altogether prior any overt manifestation becomes apparent clinico-pathologically allowing us finally achieve meaningful reductions morbidity mortality associated burden imposed society today stemming directly secondary widespread adoption proven effective prophylactic protocols tailored specific high-risk subgroups identified genetically/epigenetically susceptible based comprehensive genomic profiling efforts underway presently revolutionizing field oncogenetics broadly speaking .

To summarize:

Combining chemotherapy and immunotherapy increases survival in patients with metastatic melanoma but does not impact the incidence of the disease, keeping annual diagnoses steady as current prevention focuses on risk factor reduction before onset, whereas new treatments manage existing illness more effectively once detected. This means although some may survive far beyond expected timelines, others still fall ill every year similarly making up population statistics unchanged fundamentally driven by ongoing environmental
